title: Info ('I')
text: Info ('I') Provide Uniface with DBMS and connector characteristics, or supply logon information. Description Refer to the following modes for the Info request: Table 1. Modes Mode Description Mode 0 Provide Uniface with DBMS and connector characteristics by filling in all applicable elements of the uinfo structure. The mode 0 Info request must be successful and return 0 to Uniface. Mode 1 Provide Uniface with the user name and password corresponding to the current logon path. If the connector is not capable of providing logon information, return -1 to Uniface. From Uniface Preconditions realized by Uniface—When the Info request is called in mode 1, there is an established logon path. Table 2. Information Available from Uniface Data Structure->Element Description Modes uctrl->umisc Address of the uinfo structure. All Modes uinfo->mode Requested mode of the Info request. All Modes uctrl->udrvini Unique identification of the current logon path. Mode 1 uctrl->iobuf Points to the general I/O buffer. Mode 1 uctrl->sqlbuf Points to the SQL buffer. Mode 1 To Uniface Mode information related to the Info request, and the connector request return values. Table 3. Modes Mode Description Mode 0 All applicable elements of the uinfo structure must be given a value. Elements that are not given a value will have a default value of 0. This is an appropriate default for all non-mandatory elements. The connector request return value must be 0; do not change the value of uctrl->udrverr . Mode 1 Store the user name and password corresponding to the current logon path as two contiguous null-terminated strings in the general I/O buffer. For example: scott/0tiger/0 Any of the two strings may be empty, but the two NULL characters (‘/0’ in ‘C’) must be present. Table 4. Connector Request Return Values Value Description 0 Logon information is provided in the general I/O buffer. -1 Logon information is not available. -9 I/O server logon error. If the connector request return value is less than 0, a connector error code must be supplied in uctrl->udrverr . Service Functions Not applicable. Extra information The Info request is provided with a dummy control block. This control block does not describe a table and it does not have an associated connector workspace. Table 5. Modes Mode Description Mode 0 The Info request can be called several times. Identical characteristics must be provided with each call. No special initialization should be performed in the request. If incorrect or inconsistent values are provided to Uniface, very obscure errors can occur in further processing. Mode 1 The mode 1 Info request is called when there is a reference to $user or $password in the ProcScript language. The connector may implement the request for logon information in the following ways: Store the information in a static area when the Logon request is performed. Get the information from the DBMS. Related Links uctrl Exchanges general information between Uniface and the connector, and describes a table. uinfo Supplies Uniface with basic information about the DBMS and the connector.
